run_metadata.coverage_summary: DEGRADED RUN (headline-only) for the second consecutive day. WebFetch is fully blocked by this session's outbound egress policy: every WebFetch and every curl-through-proxy CONNECT returned HTTP 403 for ALL destination hosts, including control hosts (example.com returned 403 identically to tenders.gov.au). This is an organization egress-policy denial recorded at the proxy gateway, not per-site bot protection, and per /root/.ccr/README.md a proxy 403 must be reported rather than retried or bypassed. WebSearch (server-side, not routed through the egress proxy) remained functional and was the only working web tool. Consequently NO tender detail page or specification document could be opened this run: every tender below is HEADLINE-LEVEL ONLY (identity, buyer, closing date and — where the search index surfaced it — a genuine AusTender deep-link/ATM ID), and all deliverables/requirements/eligibility/evaluation/documents/contacts fields are null and counted in entries_pending_detail. Tier-1 Commonwealth (AusTender) yielded 6 confirmable open ATMs with real closing dates; 5 are still-open opportunities carried from earlier runs (re-reported to advance last_seen/active status) and 1 (DCET2026-01A) is newly seen. Tier-1 state/territory portals (NSW/VIC/QLD/WA/SA/TAS/NT/ACT) returned only generic landing pages via the search index — no clean fresh non-excluded state tender detail could be captured with confidence. Source discovery (mission half 2) was productive: 7 genuinely new buyer-originated Australian sources were identified via WebSearch and recorded in discovered_sources. Tier-3 corporate/GBE opportunity detail remained login/invitation-gated as expected; no corporate RFx was fabricated.
gaps: CRITICAL (2nd consecutive day): WebFetch is denied by the session outbound egress policy for ALL hosts. Every WebFetch call and every curl-through-proxy CONNECT returned HTTP 403, confirmed against control hosts example.com (403) and against tenders.gov.au (403). The proxy status endpoint reported enabled=true with no recentRelayFailures, and /root/.ccr/README.md classifies a proxy 403 as an organization egress-policy denial that must be reported and NOT retried or routed around. WebSearch was the only functioning web tool this session. Yesterday's run (2026-07-03) hit the identical block; runs on/before 2026-07-02 clearly had WebFetch access, so this is a persistent change in this session's egress allowlist, not a transient outage. ACTION NEEDED: allow outbound HTTPS to the tender portals so detail/deliverables extraction can resume.

No tender detail page or specification document could be opened this run. For every tender captured, deliverables_summary, key_deliverables, scope_of_work, requirements, eligibility, evaluation_criteria, documents, contacts, value, contract_term and publish_date could not be verified against the notice and are null/empty. All 6 tenders are headline-level only (entries_pending_detail=6). Closing dates shown were taken from WebSearch result snippets that quote the AusTender listing rows, not from an opened detail page; treat as high-but-not-verified confidence and re-run with fetch access to complete a deliverables/detail pass.

Tier-1 state/territory portals — NSW eTendering/buy.nsw, QTenders (QLD)/etender.hpw.qld.gov.au/supply.qld.gov.au, Tenders WA, Tenders SA, Tenders Tasmania, NT QTOL, Tenders ACT, Buying for Victoria — all unreachable via WebFetch (403). The search index surfaced only generic portal landing pages plus council items on aggregator/TenderLink pages; no fresh non-excluded STATE government tender detail could be captured with confidence this run.

Tier-2 (councils/GBEs/universities/health) and Tier-3 (corporate): VendorPanel public tenders and every individual buyer portal (Queensland Health, Port Authority NSW, Water Corporation WA, SAP Ariba Discovery, BHP, Rio Tinto, etc.) were unreachable via WebFetch (403). Genuine buyer-originated portal URLs were recovered from the search index and recorded in discovered_sources, but no individual opportunity could be opened or verified, and no corporate/GBE RFx was recorded as a tender.

Tier-3 corporate e-sourcing hubs (SAP Ariba Discovery/SAP Business Network Discovery, Coupa, Jaggaer, RangeMe) and major-buyer portals (BHP, Rio Tinto, Woodside, Fortescue, Santos, Woolworths, Coles, Qantas, the big-four banks, Telstra/Optus) could not be opened; per prior fetch-verified runs and search evidence their opportunity detail is login- or invitation-gated. No publicly-visible corporate RFx could be confirmed this run, so none was recorded as a tender (portals remain captured in discovered_sources with access_method=portal-registration). Invitation-only corporate RFPs (e.g. management-consultancy panels) remain out of scope and unobservable.
WebSearch is US-geolocated, which limits freshness/geo-ranking of Australian results and may miss items published in the last 24 hours that have not yet been indexed.
recommended_seed_additions: Queensland Health — Tender opportunities (health.qld.gov.au/system-governance/suppliers/information/doing-business-with-us/tender-opportunities) — state health buyer portal, complements the already-known NSW Health / HealthShare entry
Port Authority of New South Wales — Current tenders (portal.tenderlink.com/portauthority/alltenders/) — GBE buyer-originated e-tendering portal on illion TenderLink

Infrastructure NSW — Procurement Opportunities (infrastructure.nsw.gov.au/industry/procurement-and-commercial-policy/procurement-opportunities/) — major-projects buyer publishing its own upcoming/open procurements

AusTender GovPanels / Supplier Portal (help.tenders.gov.au — Central Procurement Systems Enhancements) — new Commonwealth supplier-discovery portal rolling out mid-2026 alongside AusTender; free supplier registration
Mid West Ports Authority (WA) — port GBE buyer running its own service-provider tenders
Newcastle City Council — Public tender opening list (newcastle.nsw.gov.au) — local-government buyer publishing tender registers directly
City of Wanneroo (WA) — Tenders and contracts (wanneroo.wa.gov.au/info/20003/your_wanneroo/11/tenders_and_contracts) — local-government buyer current-tenders page

discovered_sources.how_discovered: WebSearch for Australian hospital/health procurement portals; Queensland Health publishes its own tender opportunities page for system procurement, distinct from the already-known NSW Health/HealthShare entry.
discovered_sources.notes: State public-health buyer. Individual opportunity detail could not be opened this run (WebFetch 403); page identified from the search index only.

discovered_sources.source_name: Port Authority of New South Wales — Current tenders
discovered_sources.how_discovered: WebSearch for Australian port authority tender portals; Port Authority of NSW (Sydney) runs its own buyer-originated e-tendering portal on illion TenderLink (portal.tenderlink.com/<buyer>), which is buyer-originated (not an aggregator listing).
discovered_sources.notes: GBE buyer. Sibling to other TenderLink-hosted buyer portals already known (ARTC, City of Sydney, Sydney Airport). Detail not opened this run (WebFetch 403).

discovered_sources.how_discovered: WebSearch for NSW government procurement; Infrastructure NSW publishes its own procurement-opportunities page for major-project engagements.
discovered_sources.notes: Major-projects buyer. Opportunities may cross-list on NSW eTendering but the buyer publishes its own page. Detail not opened this run (WebFetch 403).

discovered_sources.how_discovered: WebSearch for new Australian government procurement portals 2026; the Commonwealth is rolling out a new Supplier Portal / GovPanels capability alongside AusTender from mid-2026 under the Central Procurement Systems Enhancements program.
discovered_sources.notes: New federal supplier-discovery portal (does not replace AusTender for tenders). Free supplier registration. Worth monitoring as it comes online in 2026.

discovered_sources.how_discovered: WebSearch for Australian port authority tenders; Mid West Ports Authority (WA GBE) was surfaced seeking service providers. Landing URL inferred as the buyer's own site; exact tenders path not confirmed (WebFetch 403).
discovered_sources.notes: WA port GBE buyer. Confidence medium — buyer confirmed, exact current-tenders URL not verified this run.

discovered_sources.source_name: Newcastle City Council — Public tender opening list
discovered_sources.how_discovered: WebSearch surfaced a Newcastle City Council public tender opening list (PDF register) directly on the council domain.
discovered_sources.notes: Local-government buyer publishing tender registers directly (in addition to any VendorPanel/TenderLink listing). Detail not opened this run (WebFetch 403).

discovered_sources.source_name: City of Wanneroo — Tenders and contracts

discovered_sources.notes: WA local-government buyer current-tenders page. Detail not opened this run (WebFetch 403).

discovered_sources.source_name: SAP Ariba Discovery / SAP Business Network Discovery
discovered_sources.how_discovered: Mandatory Tier-3 check. Public posting board where buyers post open RFx; full detail requires a free supplier account. Could not be opened this run (WebFetch 403) so no individual opportunity captured; recorded to keep the corporate source set complete.
discovered_sources.notes: Known source, re-affirmed. Opportunity detail is registration-gated; no corporate RFx fabricated. Authenticated fetch access required to read individual events.

discovered_sources.how_discovered: Mandatory Tier-3 check of a known major mining buyer. Opportunities run via SAP Ariba + GCMS (interestedsupplier.bhp.com) and are login-gated. Could not be opened this run (WebFetch 403).
discovered_sources.notes: Known source, re-affirmed to keep Tier-3 coverage explicit. Registration/invitation-gated; no opportunity captured or fabricated.
